annotations.label: Zeta function of a variety over F_q

anchor.snippet: The zeta function ζ ( X , s ) of X is by definition

annotations.note: Mathlib has no definition of the Hasse–Weil zeta function of a scheme over a finite field; only Dirichlet/Riemann/Hurwitz/Dedekind zeta functions exist.

annotations.label: Weil conjecture: Rationality

anchor.snippet: (Rationality) ζ ( X , s ) is a rational function of T

annotations.note: No zeta function of a variety over F_q is defined in Mathlib, so its rationality is not stated.

annotations.label: Weil conjecture: Functional equation and Poincaré duality

anchor.snippet: (Functional equation and Poincaré duality) The zeta function satisfies

annotations.note: The functional equation of the Hasse–Weil zeta function and its link to Poincaré duality is absent from Mathlib.

annotations.label: Weil conjecture: Riemann hypothesis

anchor.snippet: This implies that all zeros of

annotations.note: Deligne's Riemann Hypothesis for varieties over F_q is not formalized in Mathlib.

annotations.label: Weil conjecture: Betti numbers
annotations.note: Mathlib does not define Betti numbers of varieties or connect them to the zeta function.

annotations.label: Zeta function of the projective line

anchor.snippet: The simplest example (other than a point) is to take X to be the projective line

annotations.note: No computation Z(P^1, T) = 1/((1-T)(1-qT)) appears; projective space as a scheme exists only as `ProjectiveSpectrum`.

annotations.label: Zeta function of projective space

anchor.snippet: It is not much harder to do

annotations.note: No formula for the zeta function of P^n over F_q is present in Mathlib.

annotations.label: Zeta function of an elliptic curve

anchor.snippet: These give the first non-trivial cases of the Weil conjectures

annotations.note: `WeierstrassCurve.localPolynomial` defines the local factor `1 - aT + qT^2` used in the elliptic-curve L-function over a local field, but the Weil-style zeta function of an elliptic curve over F_q is not proved.

annotations.label: Zeta function of a hyperelliptic curve

anchor.snippet: As an example, consider the hyperelliptic curve

annotations.note: Hyperelliptic curves are not defined in Mathlib.

annotations.label: Abelian surface

anchor.snippet: An Abelian surface is a two-dimensional Abelian variety

annotations.note: Mathlib has no `AbelianVariety` definition, hence no notion of an abelian surface.

annotations.label: Zeta function of the Jacobian of a genus-2 curve

anchor.snippet: As an example of an Abelian surface defined over a finite field

annotations.note: Neither Jacobians of curves nor zeta functions of abelian surfaces are formalized.

annotations.label: Grothendieck's formula for the zeta function via ℓ-adic cohomology

anchor.section: Grothendieck's proofs of three of the four conjectures
anchor.snippet: he was able to prove the conjectured formula for the zeta function

annotations.note: `EllAdicCohomology` defines `H^i(X, ℤ_ℓ)` via the pro-étale site, providing infrastructure for the formula, but the Grothendieck–Lefschetz trace formula for the zeta function is not stated or proved.

annotations.label: Grothendieck's formula for the zeta function of a sheaf

anchor.section: Grothendieck's proofs of three of the four conjectures
anchor.snippet: Grothendieck proved a similar formula for the zeta function

annotations.note: The trace-formula expression for the zeta function of an ℓ-adic sheaf is not in Mathlib.

annotations.label: Pure and mixed constructible sheaves

anchor.snippet: A constructible sheaf on a variety over a finite field is called pure of weight

annotations.note: Constructible ℓ-adic sheaves and the notion of weight are not defined in Mathlib.

annotations.note: Deligne's Weil II theorem on weight bounds for higher direct images is absent from Mathlib.
